configurations.name: DD_AAS_DOTNET_EXTENSION_VERSION
configurations.description: Holds the running version of the Azure App Services Site Extension. This env var is set in the applicationHost.xdt file.

configurations.name: DD_AAS_ENABLE_CUSTOM_METRICS
configurations.description: Used in Azure App Services to force the loader to start dogstatsd (in case automatic instrumentation is disabled)

configurations.name: DD_AAS_ENABLE_CUSTOM_TRACING
configurations.description: Used in Azure App Services to force the loader to start the trace agent (in case automatic instrumentation is disabled)

configurations.name: DD_ACTION_EXECUTION_ID
configurations.description: Sets the action execution ID reported as CI metadata for AWS CodePipeline builds. This value is used as the CI job identifier when running a CodeBuild job initiated by CodePipeline.

configurations.name: DD_AGENT_FEATURE_POLLING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables polling the /info endpoint in the trace agent for feature discovery.

configurations.name: DD_AGENT_HOST
configurations.description: The host name to use to connect the Datadog agent for traces. The host name can be IPv4, IPv6, or a domain name. If DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL is specified, the value of DD_AGENT_HOST is ignored.

configurations.name: DD_API_KEY
configurations.description: To use the Datadog CLI in Azure Cloud Shell, open a cloud shell, set your API key and site in the `DD_API_KEY` and `DD_SITE` environment variables, and use `npx` to run the CLI directly:

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether API Security features are enabled. If unset, API Security is enabled by default.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_ENDPOINT_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether API endpoint definitions are collected for API catalog features. If unset, endpoint collection is enabled by default.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_ENDPOINT_COLLECTION_MESSAGE_LIMIT
configurations.description: Sets the maximum number of API endpoints included in a single endpoint-collection telemetry message. If unset, a default limit is used.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_PARSE_RESPONSE_BODY
configurations.description: Enables the parsing of the response body in the API Security module. Defaults to true

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_SAMPLE_DELAY
configurations.description: Sets the delay (in seconds) between API Security schema samples. This setting is intended for system testing and is not meant for general use.

configurations.name: DD_APM_ENABLE_RARE_SAMPLER
configurations.description: Enables rare trace sampling in the Datadog Agent. When enabled, the Agent can keep a small number of "rare" traces (up to 5 traces per second per Agent) that were not kept by head-based sampling.

configurations.name: DD_APM_RECEIVER_PORT
configurations.description: Used to force a specific port binding for the Trace Agent. Default value is 8126.

configurations.name: DD_APM_RECEIVER_SOCKET
configurations.description: Unix domain socket where the Tracer can send traces. Default value is null.

configurations.name: DD_APM_TRACING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables sending APM trace data. When disabled, tracing still runs to support other products, but only periodic heartbeat traces and manually kept traces are sent.

configurations.name: DD_APPLICATION_MONITORING_CONFIG_FILE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether reading the application monitoring config file through libdatadog (hands off config) is enabled. True by default

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_AUTOMATED_USER_EVENTS_TRACKING
configurations.description: Deprecate. Automatic tracking of user events mode. Values can be disabled, safe or extended. This config is in the process of being deprecated. Please use DD_APPSEC_AUTO_USER_INSTRUMENTATION_MODE instead. Values will be automatically translated: disabled = disabled safe = anon extended = ident

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_AUTO_USER_INSTRUMENTATION_MODE
configurations.description: Selects the AppSec automatic user instrumentation mode, which controls how user events are collected.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables the AppSec product inside the tracer. When enabled the tracer initializes the AppSec module (in-app WAF, header/body collection and AppSec telemetry)

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_EXTRA_HEADERS
configurations.description: Comma separated of additional header names (beyond the default allowlist) that the tracer should capture. This is useful when a service requires custom authentication or application-specific headers to be analysed. Extra headers should be configured with privacy considerations in mind and are typically kept only within the customer’s org; the tracer will obey redaction/obfuscation settings for header values

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HTTP_BLOCKED_TEMPLATE_HTML
configurations.description: Path to a local HTML template file that will be returned when a request is blocked and the response should be HTML. If unset or invalid, the tracer uses the default blocking HTML template defined by the product RFC. The tracer decides HTML or JSON according to `Accept` header or the block action's parameters.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HTTP_BLOCKED_TEMPLATE_JSON
configurations.description: Path to a local JSON template file that will be returned when a request is blocked and the response should be JSON. If unset or invalid, the tracer uses the default blocking HTML template defined by the product RFC. The tracer decides HTML or JSON according to `Accept` header or the block action's parameters.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_IPHEADER
configurations.description: Specify a single HTTP header to use for client IP resolution. When set, the tracer treats that header as the authoritative source of client IP and ignores the default set of candidate headers. This helps avoid ambiguity and IP spoofing issues when proxies or CDNs provide custom client IP headers; use with caution and follow deployment best practices.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_KEEP_TRACES
configurations.description: When true, the tracer marks traces that contain AppSec events (WAF triggers, RASP/IAST findings, user events) so they are retained by the sampling and ingestion pipeline. This ensures events remain available for UI analysis and backend processing even when ordinary sampling would drop the trace. For internal testing only.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_MAX_STACK_TRACES
configurations.description: Limits how many separate stacktraces are captured and attached to an AppSec/IAST event to keep payload sizes reasonable. Evidence stacktraces are useful for triage, but unbounded capture can leak sensitive data and consume excessive bandwidth; this limit balances evidence value and safety. Set to 0 to collect all.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_MAX_STACK_TRACE_DEPTH
configurations.description: Sets the maximum number of stack frames retained for any captured stacktrace attached to AppSec events. Tracers should prioritise top-of-stack frames and truncate deeper frames when this limit is reached. The setting helps keep evidentiary stacktraces compact while preserving the most actionable frames

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_MAX_STACK_TRACE_DEPTH_TOP_PERCENT
configurations.description: When the tracer must trim a stacktrace, this value controls how much of the top portion of the stacktrace is preserved at higher fidelity (a percentage). This lets implementations keep more of the top frames (most actionable) while trimming the less usefully deep frames

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_OBFUSCATION_PARAMETER_KEY_REGEXP
configurations.description: A regular expression used by the AppSec obfuscator to identify parameter keys that must cause full redaction. When any `key_path` string matches this regexp the entire parameter is replaced by `<redacted by datadog>`. The default key regexp matches common secret/key names (password, token, authorization, jwt, etc.) an empty string disables the key regexp

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_RASP_ENABLED
configurations.description: Turns on the tracer's RASP / Exploit Prevention capabilities. When enabled the tracer runs synchronous, pre-execution checks and may generate block actions or exploit signals. RASP may have separate activation and implementation details per language; enabling it usually activates extra synchronous instrumentation and additional evidence capture for detections

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_RULES
configurations.description: Override the default rules file provided. Must be a path to a valid JSON rules file

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_SCA_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the tracer's runtime Software Composition Analysis (Runtime SCA) capability, which reports runtime dependency/SBOM information and powers runtime vulnerability detection. Useful for testing and demonstrations as well as production runtime vulnerability workflows; SCA may have billing/usage implications and language compatibility constraints described in the SCA runbooks

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_STACK_TRACE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables security-related stack traces generation when security events occur.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_TRACE_RATE_LIMIT
configurations.description: Controls the maximum amount of AppSec traces, per second.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_WAF_DEBUG
configurations.description: Turns on internal debug/trace logging for the in-app WAF. This is intended for debugging and is very verbose — it can severely impact performance. Use together with `DD_APPSEC_WAF_TIMEOUT` when debugging and prefer narrow log filters on production systems

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_WAF_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Limits the WAF synchronous execution time (in microseconds).

configurations.name: DD_AZURE_APP_SERVICES
configurations.description: Configuration key which is used as a flag to tell us whether we are instrumenting an Azure App Service using the AAS Site Extension.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_AGENTLESS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Agentless mode in CI Visibility. Default value is false (disabled).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_AGENTLESS_URL
configurations.description: Overrides the intake base URL used for agentless CI test reporting. When set, payloads are sent to this URL instead of the default site-based intake URL.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_AUTO_INSTRUMENTATION_PROVIDER
configurations.description: Identifies the auto instrumentation provider for CI Visibility. This is used to track which instrumentation method was used (e.g., single-step, manual, etc.).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_CODE_COVERAGE_COLLECTORPATH
configurations.description: Path to the code coverage collector.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_CODE_COVERAGE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Code Coverage in CI Visibility.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_CODE_COVERAGE_ENABLE_JIT_OPTIMIZATIONS
configurations.description: Enables or disables JIT optimizations in Code Coverage.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_CODE_COVERAGE_MODE
configurations.description: Selects the code coverage mode: LineExecution or LineCallCount.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_CODE_COVERAGE_PATH
configurations.description: Destination path for code coverage JSON files.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_CODE_COVERAGE_SNK_FILEPATH
configurations.description: Path to the SNK file used for re-signing assemblies after Code Coverage modification.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_DI_ENABLED
configurations.description: Dependency injection for divisibility

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_EARLY_FLAKE_DETECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables the early flake detection feature in CI Visibility.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enabling or disabling CI Visibility

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_EXTERNAL_CODE_COVERAGE_PATH
configurations.description: Path to the external code coverage file.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_FLAKY_RETRY_COUNT
configurations.description: Can be set to any non-negative number to change the maximum number of retries per test case.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_FLAKY_RETRY_ENABLED
configurations.description: A kill switch for the auto test retries feature.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_FORCE_AGENT_EVP_PROXY
configurations.description: Forces the use of the Agent's EVP Proxy.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_GAC_INSTALL_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Datadog.Trace GAC installation.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_GIT_UPLOAD_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables uploading Git metadata in CI Visibility. Default Value is false (disabled).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_IMPACTED_TESTS_DETECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Impacted Tests Detection.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_ITR_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Intelligent Test Runner in CI Visibility. Default Value is false (disabled).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_KNOWN_TESTS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables the known tests feature in CI Visibility.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_LOGS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Logs direct submission. Default value is false (disabled).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_RUM_FLUSH_WAIT_MILLIS
configurations.description: CI Visibility (Selenium): after calling `window.DD_RUM.stopSession()` at the end of a browser test, waits this many milliseconds before proceeding to allow RUM data to flush (default: 500ms).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_TESTSSKIPPING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables the Intelligent Test Runner test skipping feature in CI Visibility.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_TOTAL_FLAKY_RETRY_COUNT
configurations.description: Sets the maximum total number of automatic test retries allowed across the entire test session. This limit is applied when automatic flaky-test retries are enabled and defaults to 1000.

configurations.name: DD_CODE_ORIGIN_FOR_SPANS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Code Origin for spans (Dynamic Instrumentation): starts the code-origin recorder and enables instrumentations that capture code origin at span entry (via `DebuggerContext.captureCodeOrigin`), producing code-origin snapshots/tags for spans. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_CODE_ORIGIN_FOR_SPANS_MAX_USER_FRAMES
configurations.description: Maximum number of frames to tag in exit span code origin. Default value is 8.

configurations.name: DD_CUSTOM_TRACE_ID
configurations.description: Custom variable set by the Jenkins Datadog Plugin for trace IDs.

configurations.name: DD_DATA_STREAMS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables data streams monitoring support.

configurations.name: DD_DATA_STREAMS_LEGACY_HEADERS
configurations.description: Enables legacy binary headers in Data Streams Monitoring. False by default if DSM is in default state, true otherwise.

configurations.name: DD_DBM_PROPAGATION_MODE
configurations.description: Enables linking between data sent from APM and the Database Monitoring product when set to `service` or `full`. The `service` option enables the connection between DBM and APM services. The `full` option enables connection between database spans with database query events. Available for Postgres and MySQL.

configurations.name: DD_DIAGNOSTIC_SOURCE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the use of System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource, making it possible to receive spans from libraries that self-instrument with the legacy System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource sub-system. Default value is true (enabled).

configurations.name: DD_DISABLED_INTEGRATIONS
configurations.description: List of integrations to disable. All other integrations remain enabled. Default is empty (all integrations are enabled). Supports multiple values separated with semi-colons. It's an alternative to having multiple “DD_{INTEGRATION_NAME}_ENABLED=false“

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_ARGS
configurations.description: Azure App Services: extra command-line arguments appended when the tracer starts an external DogStatsD process (only used when `DD_DOGSTATSD_PATH` is set).

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_PATH
configurations.description: Azure App Services: path to the DogStatsD executable to run as an external process. When set, the tracer starts and supervises a `dogstatsd` process (passing `DD_DOGSTATSD_ARGS`) and optionally health-checks it using `DD_DOGSTATSD_PIPE_NAME`.

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_PIPE_NAME
configurations.description: Named pipe that DogStatsD binds to. Default value is null.

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_PORT
configurations.description: The port of the DogStatsD Agent that metrics are submitted to. If the Agent configuration sets `dogstatsd_port` or `DD_DOGSTATSD_PORT` to something other than the default `8125`, then this tracing library `DD_DOGSTATSD_PORT` must match it.

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_SOCKET
configurations.description: Unix domain socket that DogStatsD binds to. Default value is null.

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_URL
configurations.description: The URL to use to connect the Datadog agent for Dogstatsd metrics.

configurations.name: DD_DOTNET_TRACER_HOME
configurations.description: Points to the home directory of the dotnet Datadog tracer. On Windows, it is often set to the installation path, such as C:\Program Files\Datadog\.NET Tracer. On Linux/Docker, it is typically set to /opt/datadog.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_DIAGNOSTICS_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Interval (seconds) at which probe diagnostic/status messages (received/installed/emitting/errors) may be re-emitted

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Dynamic Instrumentation.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_MAX_DEPTH_TO_SERIALIZE
configurations.description: Maximum object depth to serialize for probe snapshots

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_MAX_TIME_TO_SERIALIZE
configurations.description: Maximum duration (in milliseconds) to run serialization for probe snapshots.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_REDACTED_EXCLUDED_IDENTIFIERS
configurations.description: Set of identifiers that are excluded from redaction decisions.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_REDACTED_IDENTIFIERS
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of additional identifier keywords to redact in captured snapshot data

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_REDACTED_TYPES
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of object types to redact from dynamic logs and snapshots. Supports * wildcards (e.g. Secret* matches SecretKey).

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_REDACTION_EXCLUDED_IDENTIFIERS
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of identifier keywords to exclude from the default redaction list. Useful to prevent common keywords like password from being treated as sensitive for your use case.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_UPLOAD_BATCH_SIZE
configurations.description: Upload batch size (number of snapshots/diagnostic messages per request). Controls how many snapshots are serialized and sent per flush. Default: 100.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_UPLOAD_FLUSH_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Low-rate upload flush interval in milliseconds. 0 (default) enables an adaptive flush interval; any other value uses a fixed interval. Deprecated in favor of DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_UPLOAD_INTERVAL_SECONDS.

configurations.name: DD_ENV
configurations.description: The application's environment. Sets the "env" tag on every span.

configurations.name: DD_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_AGENTLESS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables agentless Exception Replay uploads. Default value is false.

configurations.name: DD_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_AGENTLESS_URL
configurations.description: When Agentless mode in Exception Replay is enabled we can use this configuration to force an URL for the intake

configurations.name: DD_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_CAPTURE_FULL_CALLSTACK_ENABLED
configurations.description: Configuration key to enable capturing the variables of all the frames in exception call stack. Default value is false.

configurations.name: DD_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_CAPTURE_MAX_FRAMES
configurations.description: Maximum number of frames in a call stack to capture values for.

configurations.name: DD_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Exception Replay. Default value is false (disabled).

configurations.name: DD_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_MAX_EXCEPTION_ANALYSIS_LIMIT
configurations.description: Exception Replay: maximum number of exceptions per second to analyze/handle (circuit breaker). When the limit is exceeded, additional exceptions are ignored for that second. Default: 100.

configurations.name: DD_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_RATE_LIMIT_SECONDS
configurations.description: Interval used to rate-limit exception tracking. Default value is 1h.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_APPSEC_USE_UNSAFE_ENCODER
configurations.description: Use new unsafe encoder for the waf

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_FLAGGING_PROVIDER_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the experimental feature-flag provider that evaluates flags from remote configuration. If not enabled, the provider is a no-op and returns default values.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_PROPAGATE_PROCESS_TAGS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables experimental propagation of process tags in payloads such as spans and telemetry.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_BRANCH
configurations.description: Overrides the Git branch value used by CI Visibility for repository and commit correlation.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_AUTHOR_DATE
configurations.description: The date when the author submitted the commit expressed in ISO 8601 format.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit author email reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_AUTHOR_NAME
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit author name reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_COMMITTER_DATE
configurations.description: The date when the committer submitted the commit expressed in ISO 8601 format.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit committer email reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_COMMITTER_NAME
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit committer name reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_MESSAGE
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit message reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_SHA
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit SHA reported as CI metadata and used for git metadata tagging when enabled.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_PULL_REQUEST_BASE_BRANCH
configurations.description: CI Visibility: sets the pull request base/target branch name, used to populate pull request info and CI git tags (for example, `git.pull_request.base_branch`) when user-supplied git metadata is needed.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_PULL_REQUEST_BASE_BRANCH_SHA
configurations.description: Overrides the pull request base commit SHA reported as git metadata for CI features. Use this to set the base commit when it cannot be detected automatically.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_REPOSITORY_URL
configurations.description: Overrides the git repository URL reported as CI metadata and used for git metadata tagging when enabled. Any embedded credentials in the URL are stripped before use.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_TAG
configurations.description: Overrides the Git tag used by CI Visibility for repository and release correlation.

configurations.name: DD_HTTP_CLIENT_ERROR_STATUSES
configurations.description: Client HTTP status codes that mark spans as errors.

configurations.name: DD_HTTP_SERVER_ERROR_STATUSES
configurations.description: Server HTTP status codes that mark spans as errors.

configurations.name: DD_HTTP_SERVER_TAG_QUERY_STRING
configurations.description: Enables or disables reporting the query string on server spans. Default value is true.

configurations.name: DD_HTTP_SERVER_TAG_QUERY_STRING_SIZE
configurations.description: Maximum size of the query string to report, before obfuscation. Default value is 5000. A value of 0 means no size limit.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_COOKIE_FILTER_PATTERN
configurations.description: Number of rows to taint on each database query in IAST. Default value is 1.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_DB_ROWS_TO_TAINT
configurations.description: IAST: maximum number of JDBC `ResultSet` rows to treat as taint sources (SQL table data) for a given `ResultSet`. Rows beyond this limit are not tainted. Default: 1.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_DEDUPLICATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables vulnerability deduplication detection. When enabled, a vulnerability is only reported once in the lifetime of an app, instead of on every occurrence. Default value is true (enabled).

configurations.name: DD_IAST_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables IAST. Default value is false (disabled).

configurations.name: DD_IAST_MAX_CONCURRENT_REQUESTS
configurations.description: Maximum number of requests to be analyzed by IAST concurrently. Default value is 2.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_MAX_RANGE_COUNT
configurations.description: IAST: maximum number of tainted ranges stored per tainted object/value. If more ranges are produced, they are truncated to this limit. Only used in `DEFAULT` detection mode; in `FULL` detection mode this is unlimited. Default (in `DEFAULT` mode): 10.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_REDACTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables IAST redaction of sensitive data. Default value is in TracerSettings.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_REDACTION_NAME_PATTERN
configurations.description: Custom regex pattern to obfuscate source keys in IAST. Default value is in TracerSettings.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_REDACTION_VALUE_PATTERN
configurations.description: Custom regex pattern to obfuscate source values in IAST. Default value is in TracerSettings.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_REGEXP_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Timeout in milliseconds for regex execution in IAST. Default value is 200ms.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_REQUEST_SAMPLING
configurations.description: Percentage of requests to be analyzed by IAST, between 1 and 100. Default value is 30.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_TELEMETRY_VERBOSITY
configurations.description: Specifies the verbosity of the sent telemetry. Default 'INFORMATION'

configurations.name: DD_IAST_TRUNCATION_MAX_VALUE_LENGTH
configurations.description: Default value is 250

configurations.name: DD_IAST_VULNERABILITIES_PER_REQUEST
configurations.description: IAST: maximum number of vulnerabilities that can be reported per request (quota/overhead control). In `FULL` detection mode this limit is removed; in `DEFAULT` detection mode the default is 2.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_WEAK_CIPHER_ALGORITHMS
configurations.description: Controls which weak cipher algorithms are reported.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_WEAK_HASH_ALGORITHMS
configurations.description: Controls which weak hashing algorithms are reported.

configurations.name: DD_INJECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Single Step Instrumentation (SSI): marker/metadata indicating the tracer was injected by SSI (and potentially which components were injected). When present, the bootstrap sets the instrumentation source to `ssi`, and other subsystems may use the value (for example, profiling treats it as injected when it contains `profiler`).

configurations.name: DD_INJECT_FORCE
configurations.description: SSI variable that allows unsupported runtimes to be instrumented. Used for correlation purposes in telemetry.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_INSTALL_ID
configurations.description: Sets the install ID included in instrumentation telemetry payloads and headers. This can be used to correlate telemetry to a specific installation.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_INSTALL_TIME
configurations.description: Sets the install time included in instrumentation telemetry payloads and headers.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_INSTALL_TYPE
configurations.description: Sets the install type included in instrumentation telemetry payloads and headers.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_TELEMETRY_AGENTLESS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Sends telemetry directly to the intake when enabled and an API key is set. If the agent is unavailable, telemetry is sent directly to intake. Enabled by default when an API key is available.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_TELEMETRY_AGENT_PROXY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Sends telemetry via the agent proxy when enabled. Enabled by default. If disabled, or if the agent is unavailable, telemetry is sent to the agentless endpoint based on CI Visibility agentless settings.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_TELEMETRY_COMPRESSION_METHOD
configurations.description: Configuration key to allow telemetry compression.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_TELEMETRY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Datadog may collect environmental and diagnostic information about your system to improve the product. When false, this telemetry data will not be collected. **Default**: true

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_TELEMETRY_URL
configurations.description: URL where the tracer sends telemetry. Only applies when agentless telemetry is in use; otherwise telemetry is sent to the agent.

configurations.name: DD_INTERNAL_PROFILING_NATIVE_ENGINE_PATH
configurations.description: This variable is set by the native loader after trying to load the profiler. It means the profiler is _there_ though it may not be _loaded_. This only works on Windows at the moment. We assume that the CLR profiler must be attached in this scenario.

configurations.name: DD_INTERNAL_RCM_POLL_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Controls how often remote configuration is polled, in seconds. Values must be greater than 0 and no more than 5.

configurations.name: DD_INTERNAL_TELEMETRY_DEBUG_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables debug mode for telemetry.

configurations.name: DD_INTERNAL_TRACE_NATIVE_ENGINE_PATH
configurations.description: This variable is set by the native loader after trying to load the profiler. It means the profiler is _there_ though it may not be _loaded_. This only works on Windows at the moment. We assume that the CLR profiler must be attached in this scenario.

configurations.name: DD_INTERNAL_WAIT_FOR_DEBUGGER_ATTACH
configurations.description: Enables or disables the Tracer's debugger attach mode. Default value is false (disabled).

configurations.name: DD_INTERNAL_WAIT_FOR_NATIVE_DEBUGGER_ATTACH
configurations.description: Enables or disables the Tracer's native debugger attach mode. Default value is false (disabled).

configurations.name: DD_LOGS_DIRECT_SUBMISSION_AZURE_FUNCTIONS_HOST_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables direct log submission to the Datadog backend for Azure Functions hosts

configurations.name: DD_LOGS_DIRECT_SUBMISSION_BATCH_PERIOD_SECONDS
configurations.description: Time to wait between checking for log batches. Default value is 2s.

configurations.name: DD_LOGS_DIRECT_SUBMISSION_HOST
configurations.description: Set the name of the originating host for direct logs submission. Required for direct logs submission (default is machine name).

configurations.name: DD_LOGS_DIRECT_SUBMISSION_INTEGRATIONS
configurations.description: List of direct log submission integrations to enable. Only selected integrations are enabled for direct log submission. Default is empty (direct log submission disabled). Supports multiple values separated with semi-colons.

configurations.name: DD_LOGS_DIRECT_SUBMISSION_MAX_BATCH_SIZE
configurations.description: Maximum number of logs to send at one time. Default value is 1,000, the maximum accepted by the Datadog log API.

configurations.name: DD_LOGS_DIRECT_SUBMISSION_MAX_QUEUE_SIZE
configurations.description: Maximum number of logs to hold in the internal queue at any one time. Default value is 100,000.

configurations.name: DD_LOGS_DIRECT_SUBMISSION_MINIMUM_LEVEL
configurations.description: Minimum level logs should have to be sent to the intake. Default value is Information. Should be one of Verbose,Debug,Information,Warning,Error,Fatal.

configurations.name: DD_LOGS_DIRECT_SUBMISSION_SOURCE
configurations.description: Set the originating source for direct logs submission. Default is 'csharp'

configurations.name: DD_LOGS_DIRECT_SUBMISSION_TAGS
configurations.description: List of tags applied globally to all directly submitted logs. Supports multiple key-value pairs separated by commas, with keys and values separated by colons (for example `Key1:Value1,Key2:Value2`). If not provided, global tags are used instead.

configurations.name: DD_LOGS_DIRECT_SUBMISSION_URL
configurations.description: URL to send logs to. Default value uses the domain set in ConfigurationKeys.Site, so defaults to https://http-intake.logs.datadoghq.com:443.

configurations.name: DD_LOGS_INJECTION
configurations.description: **Supported Input**: Boolean (`true`/`false`) **Caveats**: Not supported in C++ or Go. **Description**: Enables or disables the automatic injection of trace context (trace ID, span ID) into JSON/structured application logs. This allows for correlation between traces and logs.

configurations.name: DD_LOGS_OTEL_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables support for collecting and exporting logs generated by the the OpenTelemetry Logs API. This feature is available starting with .NET 3.1 when using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging

configurations.name: DD_LOG_LEVEL
configurations.description: Sets the Datadog Agent logging level. Valid values are trace, debug, info, warn, error, critical, or off.

configurations.name: DD_MAX_LOGFILE_SIZE
configurations.description: Approximate maximum size, in bytes, for Tracer log files. Default value is 10 MB.

configurations.name: DD_MAX_TRACES_PER_SECOND
configurations.description: Maximum number of traces allowed to be submitted per second.

configurations.name: DD_METRICS_OTEL_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables OpenTelemetry metrics export. Metrics are disabled by default and are only enabled when this is set to a truthy value.

configurations.name: DD_METRICS_OTEL_METER_NAMES
configurations.description: List of meters to add to the metrics exporter for the experimental OpenTelemetry Metrics API support.

configurations.name: DD_PIPELINE_EXECUTION_ID
configurations.description: Sets the pipeline execution ID reported as CI metadata for AWS CodePipeline builds. This value is used as the CI pipeline identifier when running a CodeBuild job initiated by CodePipeline.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_CODEHOTSPOTS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables code hotspots collection for the continuous profiler. When disabled, profiling data is not linked to tracing context for hotspot attribution.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ENABLED
configurations.description: If set to `true`, enables the profiler. Defaults to `false`.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ENDPOINT_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether spans add a pprof label with the top-level request endpoint so profiles can be grouped by endpoint. Defaults to true.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_MANAGED_ACTIVATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Safety kill switch to be able to deactivate hotstandby mode for profiling in of a support case

configurations.name: DD_PROXY_HTTPS
configurations.description: Configuration key to set a proxy server for https requests.

configurations.name: DD_PROXY_NO_PROXY
configurations.description: Configuration key to set a list of hosts that should bypass the proxy. The list is space-separated.

configurations.name: DD_REMOTE_CONFIGURATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enable or disable remote configuration. Also accepts the alias `DD_REMOTE_CONFIG_ENABLED`.

configurations.name: DD_REMOTE_CONFIG_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS
configurations.description: Remote configuration polling interval in seconds.

configurations.name: DD_RUNTIME_METRICS_DIAGNOSTICS_METRICS_API_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the .NET runtime metrics collector which uses the System.Diagnostics.Metrics API instead of the EventListener based API

configurations.name: DD_RUNTIME_METRICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables the collection of runtime metrics (such as garbage collection stats, memory usage, and thread counts) for the application.

configurations.name: DD_SERVICE
configurations.description: Default service name for the application. Used as the service name for top-level spans, and used to determine the service name of some child spans.

configurations.name: DD_SITE
configurations.description: Sets the site domain used to build agentless intake URLs. If unset, a default site is used.

configurations.name: DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES
configurations.description: Span sampling: JSON rules used to decide per-span sampling priority (single-span sampling). If both `DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES` and `DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES_FILE` are set, the file setting is ignored. Rules can match spans by service/name and apply a sample rate and optional max-per-second limit.

configurations.name: DD_SYMBOL_DATABASE_BATCH_SIZE_BYTES
configurations.description: Maximum symbol size to upload, in bytes. Default value is 1 MB.

configurations.name: DD_SYMBOL_DATABASE_COMPRESSION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables compression for the symbols payload. Default value is true (enabled).

configurations.name: DD_SYMBOL_DATABASE_THIRD_PARTY_DETECTION_EXCLUDES
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of libraries to exclude from third-party detection in the symbol database. Default value is empty.

configurations.name: DD_SYMBOL_DATABASE_THIRD_PARTY_DETECTION_INCLUDES
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of libraries to include in third-party detection in the symbol database. Default value is empty.

configurations.name: DD_SYMBOL_DATABASE_UPLOAD_ENABLED
configurations.description: Symbol Database (SymDB): enables uploading symbol information (for Live Debugging / Dynamic Instrumentation). Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_TAGS
configurations.description: Custom tags applied to traces, provided as comma-separated key:value pairs (e.g. `layer:api,team:intake`).

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_DEPENDENCY_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Telemetry: enables the dependency collection service that detects application dependencies via classloading and reports them. Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Sets the interval for the telemetry status heartbeat (in seconds)

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_LOG_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: **Default**: true : Enable or disable log collection for telemetry.

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_METRICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether instrumentation telemetry sends internal metrics. Defaults to true.

configurations.name: DD_TESTSESSION_COMMAND
configurations.description: Specifies the test session command that was executed. This is used to track the command line that initiated the test session.

configurations.name: DD_TESTSESSION_WORKINGDIRECTORY
configurations.description: Specifies the test session working directory. This is used to track the working directory where the test session was executed.

configurations.name: DD_TEST_MANAGEMENT_ATTEMPT_TO_FIX_RETRIES
configurations.description: CI Visibility Test Management: overrides the number of retry executions for tests marked as "attempt to fix". If unset, the value from backend/remote settings is used.

configurations.name: DD_TEST_MANAGEMENT_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables the Test Management feature.

configurations.name: DD_TEST_SESSION_NAME
configurations.description: If `DD_TEST_SESSION_NAME` is not specified, the default value used is a combination of the:

configurations.name: DD_THIRD_PARTY_DETECTION_EXCLUDES
configurations.description: Alias of `DD_THIRD_PARTY_EXCLUDES`. Live Debugging / Exception Replay: package prefixes to treat as first-party (exclude from the third-party library filter). This allowlist overrides the default third-party library list.

configurations.name: DD_THIRD_PARTY_DETECTION_INCLUDES
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of libraries to include in third-party detection. Default value is empty.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_128_BIT_TRACEID_GENERATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether new traces use 128-bit W3C trace IDs (32-character hexadecimal strings) or 64-bit Datadog trace IDs (16-character hexadecimal strings). The default is true to support W3C trace context propagation.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_128_BIT_TRACEID_LOGGING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether 128-bit trace IDs are logged in their full 32-character format or truncated to 16 characters. Set to false for compatibility with systems that expect the shorter format.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ACTIVITY_LISTENER_ENABLED
configurations.description: Configures OTel Traces APIs by enabling the activity listener.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AGENT_ARGS
configurations.description: Azure App Services: extra command-line arguments for launching the external `trace-agent` process (whitespace/comma-separated). If unset, no extra args are passed.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AGENT_PATH
configurations.description: Azure App Services: path to the external `trace-agent` executable to launch. If unset, the tracer will not start the external trace-agent process.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AGENT_PORT
configurations.description: The port of the Trace Agent that the tracer submits to. If the Agent configuration sets receiver_port or DD_APM_RECEIVER_PORT to something other than the default 8126, then DD_TRACE_AGENT_PORT or DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL must match it.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL
configurations.description: The URL for connecting the tracer to the Datadog agent. Valid URL schemas include http:// and unix:// (UNIX Domain Sockets). This value takes precedence over DD_AGENT_HOST and DD_TRACE_AGENT_PORT if set.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Trace Analytics by default. When enabled for an integration, instrumentations set the `analytics.sample_rate` metric on spans (using the configured per-integration sample rate). Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AWS_ADD_SPAN_POINTERS
configurations.description: Enables or disables span pointers on AWS requests. Default value is true

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AZURE_EVENTHUBS_BATCH_LINKS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables instrumentation for azure eventhubs batch links. When disabled, spans for azure eventhubs batch links operations are not created.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AZURE_SERVICEBUS_BATCH_LINKS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables instrumentation for azure servicebus batch links. When disabled, spans for azure servicebus batch links operations are not created.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_BAGGAGE_MAX_BYTES
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the maximum number of bytes that can be injected into the baggage header when propagating to a downstream service. Default value is 8192 bytes.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_BAGGAGE_MAX_ITEMS
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the maximum number of items that can be injected into the baggage header when propagating to a downstream service. Default value is 64 items.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_BAGGAGE_TAG_KEYS
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of baggage keys to copy into span tags. Set to * to tag all baggage items; if unset, a default allowlist is used.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_BATCH_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Sets the batch interval in milliseconds for the serialization queue. Set to 0 to disable.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_BUFFER_SIZE
configurations.description: Internal dd-trace-dotnet span buffer configuration. Sets the size in bytes of the trace buffer.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_BYPASS_HTTP_REQUEST_URL_CACHING_ENABLED
configurations.description: When enabled, the request URL is built without using the cached HttpRequest.Url value to avoid triggering URL caching behavior. This can prevent issues in environments where caching the URL early can affect application behavior.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_CLIENT_IP_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables the automatic collection of client IP addresses from HTTP request headers. When enabled, the IP address is stored in the http.client_ip span tag.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_CLIENT_IP_HEADER
configurations.description: Configures a custom header name from which to source the http.client_ip tag value. If this variable is set, all other IP-related headers are ignored (for example, setting DD_TRACE_CLIENT_IP_HEADER=custom-ip-header and including the header custom-ip-header: 5.6.7.9 in a request results in a span tagged with "http.client_ip": "5.6.7.9"). If an empty string or null value is passed, IP headers are queried in this order: - x-forwarded-for - x-real-ip - true-client-ip - x-client-ip - x-forwarded - forwarded-for - x-cluster-client-ip - fastly-client-ip - cf-connecting-ip - cf-connecting-ipv6

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_COMMANDS_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether command-line details are collected for process execution instrumentation. When enabled, the executable and arguments may be captured (truncated as needed); when disabled, command-line details are omitted.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_CONFIG_FILE
configurations.description: Path to the configuration file. Can only be set with an environment variable or in the app.config/web.config file.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DATA_PIPELINE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Use libdatadog data pipeline to send traces. Default value is false (disabled).

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DEBUG
configurations.description: Enable debug logging in the tracer. Use OTEL_LOG_LEVEL as an alias

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DEBUG_LOOKUP_FALLBACK
configurations.description: Forces the automatic instrumentation to only use the fallback method lookup mechanism.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DEBUG_LOOKUP_MDTOKEN
configurations.description: Forces the automatic instrumentation to only use the mdToken method lookup mechanism.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DELAY_WCF_INSTRUMENTATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the updated WCF instrumentation that delays execution until later in the WCF pipeline when the WCF server exception handling is established.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DISABLED_ACTIVITY_SOURCES
configurations.description: Specifies a list of ActivitySource names (supports globbing) that will be disabled. Default is empty (all ActivitySources will be subscribed to by default). Disabling ActivitySources may break distributed tracing if those Activities are used to propagate trace context. Supports multiple values separated with commas. For example: "SomeGlob.*.PatternSource,Some.Specific.Source" When the tracer doesn't subscribe to an ActivitySource, we will NOT propagate the trace context from those Activities (we don't see them anymore). This means that distributed tracing flows that rely on these Activities for context propagation will break and cause disconnected traces. Potential impact on distributed tracing: Service A -> Ignored Activity -> Service B Creates a single trace with Service A as root and Service B as child Service A -> Disabled Activity -> Service B Creates TWO separate traces with Service A and Service B each as root spans

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DISABLED_ADONET_COMMAND_TYPES
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of ADO.NET CommandType names that should not have spans created for them. "InterceptableDbCommand" and "ProfiledDbCommand" are always disabled by default.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ENABLED
configurations.description: **Default**: true Enable web framework and library instrumentation. When false, the application code doesn't generate any traces. See also DD_APM_TRACING_ENABLED.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_EXPAND_ROUTE_TEMPLATES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether route parameters in ASP.NET and ASP.NET Core resource names should be expanded with their values. Only applies when route-template resource names are enabled.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_EXPERIMENTAL_FEATURES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Configuration key to enable experimental features.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_GIT_METADATA_ENABLED
configurations.description: Git metadata: when enabled, adds git repository URL and commit SHA tags to the root span (used for linking traces to source code). Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_GRAPHQL_ERROR_EXTENSIONS
configurations.description: Specifies which GraphQL error extensions to capture. A comma-separated list of extension keys to capture. Empty or not present means no extensions are captured.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_GRPC_TAGS
configurations.description: Maps GRPC metadata keys to tag names. Automatically applies GRPC metadata values as tags on traces.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HEADER_TAGS
configurations.description: Automatically apply specified HTTP headers as span tags. If a custom tag name is not specified, the tag key defaults to http.request.headers. for request headers and http.response.headers. for response headers.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HEADER_TAG_NORMALIZATION_FIX_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables a fix for header tags normalization. We used to normalize tag names even if they were specified in user configuration, but we should not. Default value is true.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HTTP_CLIENT_ERROR_STATUSES
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of HTTP status codes and ranges to treat as errors for HTTP client spans (for example, 400-499,500). When set, matching responses mark the span as an error.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HTTP_CLIENT_EXCLUDED_URL_SUBSTRINGS
configurations.description: Specifies which URLs are skipped by the tracer.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HTTP_SERVER_ERROR_STATUSES
configurations.description: A range of errors can be accepted. By default 5xx status codes are reported as errors for http servers. This configuration overrides that. Ex. `dd.trace.http.server.error.statuses=500,502-599`

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_INFERRED_PROXY_SERVICES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Inferred proxy services: when enabled, registers the inferred-proxy propagator to extract `x-dd-proxy*` headers and start an inferred proxy span (currently supports `x-dd-proxy=aws-apigateway`) as a parent of the server span, using header values for service/resource/tags. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_INJECT_CONTEXT_INTO_STORED_PROCEDURES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the injection of the Datadog trace context into stored procedures. Default value is false (disabled). When enabled, Datadog trace context will be injected into individual stored procedure calls when the following requirements are met: - The database is Microsoft SQL Server and TracerSettings.DbmPropagationMode is set to service or full. - The stored procedure call does not have Output, InputOutput, or Return ADO.NET command parameters.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_KAFKA_CREATE_CONSUMER_SCOPE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the creation of a span context on exiting a successful Kafka Consumer.Consume() call, and closing the scope on entering Consumer.Consume(). Default value is true (enabled).

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_LOGFILE_RETENTION_DAYS
configurations.description: Sets the number of days after which log files are deleted based on their last write-time date.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_LOGGING_RATE
configurations.description: Sets the number of seconds between identical log messages for Tracer log files. Default value is 0 and setting to 0 disables rate limiting.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_LOG_DIRECTORY
configurations.description: Sets the directory for .NET Tracer logs. Overrides the value in Datadog.Trace.Configuration.ConfigurationKeys.TraceLogPath if present. Default value is "%ProgramData%"\Datadog .NET Tracer\logs\" on Windows or "/var/log/datadog/dotnet/" on Linux.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_LOG_PATH
configurations.description: Deprecated, use DD_TRACE_LOG_DIRECTORY instead, and make sure it is a directory and not a file path

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_LOG_SINKS
configurations.description: Specifies locations to write internal diagnostic logs. Currently only file is supported. Defaults to file.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_METHODS
configurations.description: Enables automatic instrumentation on specified methods. Default value is "" (disabled).

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_METRICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables internal metrics sent to DogStatsD. Default value is false (disabled).

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_OBFUSCATION_QUERY_STRING_REGEXP
configurations.description: Applies a regex to redact sensitive data from query strings on incoming HTTP requests. The default regex matches various sensitive data patterns, including passwords, tokens, API keys, private keys, and authorization terms. Matches are replaced with <redacted>. If an empty string is passed, no obfuscation occurs. The resulting value is reported in the http.url tag.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_OBFUSCATION_QUERY_STRING_REGEXP_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Sets the timeout in milliseconds for the query string obfuscation regex execution. Default value is 200ms.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_OTEL_ENABLED
configurations.description: When true, OpenTelemetry-based tracing for custom instrumentation is enabled.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PARTIAL_FLUSH_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables incrementally flushing large traces to the Datadog Agent, reducing the chance of rejection by the Agent. Use only when you have long-lived traces or traces with many spans.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PARTIAL_FLUSH_MIN_SPANS
configurations.description: Number of spans before partially exporting a trace. This prevents keeping all the spans in memory for very large traces.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PEER_SERVICE_DEFAULTS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Peer service defaults (Naming Schema v0): when enabled, computes default `peer.service` values for eligible spans using the v1 peer-service default algorithm; when disabled (default), Naming Schema v0 does not compute peer.service defaults.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PEER_SERVICE_MAPPING
configurations.description: Maps detected peer service names to normalized values before they are attached to spans.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PIPE_NAME
configurations.description: Datadog Agent named pipe: sets the Windows named pipe name/path used as the HTTP transport to communicate with the Datadog Agent (instead of TCP or a Unix domain socket). When set, the tracer's HTTP client uses a NamedPipe socket factory. Default: unset.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PIPE_TIMEOUT_MS
configurations.description: Sets the timeout in milliseconds for named pipes communication. Default value is 0.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_BEHAVIOR_EXTRACT
configurations.description: Specifies how incoming distributed tracing headers should be handled at a service level. Accepted values are: `continue`: The SDK will continue the distributed trace if the incoming distributed tracing headers represent a valid trace context. `restart`: The SDK will always start a new trace. If the incoming distributed tracing headers represent a valid trace context, that trace context will be represented as a span link on service entry spans (as opposed to the parent span in the `continue` configuration). `ignore`: The SDK will always start a new trace and all incoming distributed tracing headers are ignored.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_EXTRACT_FIRST
configurations.description: When set to true, stops extracting after the first successful trace context extraction.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_EXTRACT
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of header formats from which to attempt to extract distributed tracing propagation data. The first format found with complete and valid headers is used to define the trace to continue.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_INJECT
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of header formats to include to propagate distributed traces between services.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RATE_LIMIT
configurations.description: Sets the maximum number of traces to sample per second; applies only when either DD_TRACE_SAMPLING_RULES or DD_TRACE_SAMPLE_RATE is set.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REMOVE_INTEGRATION_SERVICE_NAMES_ENABLED
configurations.description: When enabled, disables inferred-services/integration service naming by making Naming Schema v0 not allow inferred services (so integrations that rely on inferred services fall back to application service naming)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ROUTE_TEMPLATE_RESOURCE_NAMES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Dotnet web server instrumentation. Feature Flag: enables updated resource names on `aspnet.request`, `aspnet-mvc.request`, `aspnet-webapi.request`, and `aspnet_core.request` spans. Enables `aspnet_core_mvc.request` spans and additional features on `aspnet_core.request` spans.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Controls the ingestion sample rate (between 0.0 and 1.0) between the Agent and the backend. Use OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER as an alias

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SAMPLING_RULES
configurations.description: Semi-colon separated list of sampling rules. The rule is matched in order of specification. The first match in a list is used. Per entry: The item "sample_rate" is required in decimal format. The item "service" is optional in regular expression format, to match on service name. The item "name" is optional in regular expression format, to match on operation name. To give a rate of 50% to any traces in a service starting with the text "cart": '[{"sample_rate":0.5, "service":"cart.*"}]' To give a rate of 20% to any traces which have an operation name of "http.request": '[{"sample_rate":0.2, "name":"http.request"}]' To give a rate of 100% to any traces within a service named "background" and with an operation name of "sql.query": '[{"sample_rate":1.0, "service":"background", "name":"sql.query"}] To give a rate of 10% to all traces '[{"sample_rate":0.1}]' To configure multiple rules, separate by semi-colon and order from most specific to least specific: '[{"sample_rate":0.5, "service":"cart.*"}, {"sample_rate":0.2, "name":"http.request"}, {"sample_rate":1.0, "service":"background", "name":"sql.query"}, {"sample_rate":0.1}]' If no rules are specified, or none match, default internal sampling logic will be used.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SAMPLING_RULES_FORMAT
configurations.description: Sets the format of custom sampling rules. Valid values are regex or glob. If the value is not recognized, trace sampling rules are disabled.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVICE_MAPPING
configurations.description: Maps service names to replacement names for renaming.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SINGLE_SPAN_ASPNETCORE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Dotnet web server instrumentation. Enables generating only a single span in the ASP.NET Core integration, by only creating `aspnet_core.request` spans, without creating handler spans or tracking specific route values. Not compatible with DD_TRACE_ROUTE_TEMPLATE_RESOURCE_NAMES_ENABLED (that value is ignored), but may be used with DD_TRACE_EXPAND_ROUTE_TEMPLATES_ENABLED. Only supported on .NET 6+

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPAN_ATTRIBUTE_SCHEMA
configurations.description: Sets the schema version for service naming and span attributes. Accepted values are: "v1", "v0". Default value is "v0".

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_STARTUP_LOGS
configurations.description: Enable startup configuration and diagnostic log.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_STATS_COMPUTATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace-stats computation in the tracer for stats payload generation and ingestion-side metrics.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WCF_RESOURCE_OBFUSCATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Feature flag to enable obfuscating the LocalPath of a WCF request that goes into the resourceName of a span. Note: that this only applies when the WCF action is an empty string.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WCF_WEB_HTTP_RESOURCE_NAMES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Configuration key to enable or disable improved template-based resource names when using WCF Web HTTP. Requires delayed WCF instrumentation to be enabled. Enabled by default.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_X_DATADOG_TAGS_MAX_LENGTH
configurations.description: Maximum length of the `x-datadog-tags` header for trace tag propagation. Set to 0 to disable.

configurations.name: DD_VERSION
configurations.description: Your application version, set as the version tag on traces.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the OTLP endpoint URL (fallback for metrics-specific endpoint). Used when ExporterOtlpMetricsEndpoint is not set. Expects values like `unix:///path/to/socket.sock` for UDS, `\\.\pipename\` for Windows Named Pipes. Default values: gRPC: http://localhost:4317, HTTP: http://localhost:4318

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS
configurations.description: Configuration key to set custom headers for OTLP export (fallback for metrics-specific headers). Used when metrics-specific OTLP headers are not set. Format: api-key=key,other=value.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: Endpoint URL for log data only, with an optionally-specified port number. Typically ends with v1/logs when using OTLP/HTTP.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_HEADERS
configurations.description: A map of headers to apply to all outgoing otlp logs.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_PROTOCOL
configurations.description: Specifies the OTLP transport protocol to be used for log data.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: The timeout value for all outgoing logs in milliseconds.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the OTLP endpoint URL for metrics. Takes precedence over ExporterOtlpEndpoint. This value typically ends with v1/metrics when using OTLP/HTTP. Expects values like `unix:///path/to/socket.sock` for UDS, `\\.\pipename\` for Windows Named Pipes. Default values: gRPC: http://localhost:4317, HTTP: http://localhost:4318/v1/metrics

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_HEADERS
configurations.description: Configuration key to set custom headers for OTLP metrics export. Takes precedence over general OTLP headers. Format: api-key=key,other=value.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_PROTOCOL
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the OTLP protocol for metrics export. Takes precedence over the general OTLP protocol setting. Valid values: grpc, http/protobuf, http/json. Default: http/protobuf.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_TEMPORALITY_PREFERENCE
configurations.description: Controls metrics aggregation temporality. Accepted values: `delta` or `cumulative` (case-insensitive); empty/invalid values are treated as `delta`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the request timeout for OTLP metrics export in milliseconds. Takes precedence over the general OTLP timeout setting. Default value is 10000ms.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL
configurations.description: Used as the fallback OTLP protocol for metrics when `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_PROTOCOL` is unset. Parsed values: `grpc`, `http/protobuf`, `http/json` (case-insensitive). Note: `http/json` is currently unsupported for metrics and disables metrics export; `grpc`/`http/protobuf` require the corresponding Cargo features (`metrics-grpc`/`metrics-http`).

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the request timeout for OTLP export (fallback for metrics-specific timeout). Used when the metrics-specific OTLP timeout is not set. Default value is 10000ms.

configurations.name: OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER
configurations.description: Datadog SDKs that support OTLP log export should default to otlp; all others should default to None/null.

configurations.name: OTEL_LOG_LEVEL
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the log level.

configurations.name: OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER
configurations.description: Specifies the metrics exporter to be used. **Notes**: The only accepted values are `otlp` and `none`. A value of `none` disables the emission of OTel metrics, as well as APM runtime metrics (equivalent to `DD_RUNTIME_METRICS_ENABLED=false`)

configurations.name: OTEL_METRIC_EXPORT_INTERVAL
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies the time interval (in milliseconds) between metric export attempts. **Default**: 10000 (10s) **Notes**: This default value is Datadog's recommended configuration and differs from the OpenTelemetry specification's default of 60000ms.

configurations.name: OTEL_METRIC_EXPORT_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies the maximum allowed time (in milliseconds) to collect and export metrics. **Default**: 7500 (7.5s) **Notes**: This default value is Datadog's recommended configuration and differs from the OpenTelemetry specification's default of 30000ms.

configurations.name: OTEL_PROPAGATORS
configurations.description: Configures which propagators are used for extracting and injecting trace context.

configurations.name: OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES
configurations.description: Set OpenTelemetry resource attributes as a list of `(key, value)` pairs.

configurations.name: OTEL_SDK_DISABLED
configurations.description: Disables the OpenTelemetry APIs.

configurations.name: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the application's default service name.

configurations.name: OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the exporter for traces. Only the value `none` is recognized, which is equivalent to setting trace export to disabled.

configurations.name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER
configurations.description: Sets the trace sampler via OpenTelemetry configuration and maps it to the tracer's sample rate when the corresponding setting is not set. Parent-based samplers are supported; unsupported values are ignored.

configurations.name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG
configurations.description: Configuration key to set an additional argument for the traces sampler. to false.

configurations.name: _DD_EXTENSION_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: The _DD_EXTENSION_ENDPOINT isn't a standard, globally recognized AWS environment variable or service, but likely refers to a custom or specific endpoint for a DataDog (DD) integration/extension within AWS, often used in Lambda functions or containerized apps via ECS/Fargate to send metrics/logs to Datadog's services (Agent/API), especially when using Datadog's AWS integrations or extensions for seamless observability

configurations.name: _DD_EXTENSION_PATH
configurations.description: String constants for configuring the dotnet tracer with aws platform

configurations.name: _DD_INTERNAL_IS_RUNNING_IN_CIVISIBILITY
configurations.description: An internal key used to "tell" tracer settings that we're running in CI Visibility mode

configurations.name: _DD_INTERNAL_TOPT_RUNID
configurations.description: An internal key used to share a runtime ID across all the processes started in a test session

configurations.name: _DD_TRACE_STATS_COMPUTATION_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Sets the interval in seconds for sending stats (aka trace metrics).
